title: T-129 — User Menu Language Picker (SET-100)
prompt:
# T-129 — User Menu Language Picker (SET-100)

## Goal
Build SET-100 User Menu Language Picker so users can switch the UI language from the global header without a page reload, persisting Phase 1 language choices to `users.language` and respecting next-intl runtime switching.

Implement spec-driven from `prototypes/design/02-SETTINGS-UX.md SET-100` and PRD §14.2/§14.4. Phase 2 languages appear disabled/coming-soon unless the app already supports them.

## Files in scope
- apps/web/app/_components/user-menu-language-picker.tsx [create/modify]
- apps/web/app/_components/__tests__/user-menu-language-picker.test.tsx [create/modify]
- apps/web/lib/i18n/user-language.ts [create/modify]
- apps/web/lib/i18n/__tests__/user-language.test.ts [create/modify]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given the user menu opens, then active language is marked with a check and Phase 1 choices PL/EN are selectable; Phase 2 UK/RO choices are disabled or clearly marked unavailable unless supported.
- Given PL or EN is selected, when the action succeeds, then `users.language` is updated for the current user/org, next-intl switches without a full reload, and a success/error state is shown.
- Given a user attempts to force an unsupported locale, when the server action runs, then it returns a typed blocker and leaves the existing language unchanged.
- Given organizations have a locale fallback, when `users.language` is null, then the picker displays the effective fallback while still allowing user override.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: RTL/unit tests for menu options, active checkmark, disabled Phase 2 entries, update success/error, unsupported locale blocker, and fallback display.
- GREEN: build picker/action using existing next-intl and user profile primitives.
- Run targeted i18n/component tests plus typecheck/lint for changed package.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement Phase 2 language content as part of this task.
- Do not require full page reload for PL/EN switching.
- Do not update organization locale when only user preference is changed.
- Do not hard-code language state outside the app i18n primitives.
## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact test commands and outputs (RED -> GREEN, typecheck, lint)
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC: pass/fail)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

prd_refs: §14.2 i18n Phase 1 PL + EN and user preference override
§14.4 UI surfaces SET-100 User Menu Language Picker
pipeline_inputs.description: SET-100 User Menu Language Picker with user-level language persistence and next-intl hot switching.
pipeline_inputs.details: Spec-driven UI from `prototypes/design/02-SETTINGS-UX.md SET-100` and PRD §14.2/§14.4. Phase 1 supports PL and EN. UK/RO are Phase 2 and should not be enabled unless the app already provides those locales.
acceptance_criteria: User menu language picker shows active checkmark, selectable PL/EN and disabled/unavailable Phase 2 choices unless supported.
Selecting PL/EN updates users.language for the current user/org and switches next-intl without full reload.
Unsupported forced locales return typed blockers without changing existing language.
Null users.language displays the effective organization/EN fallback while allowing user override.
test_strategy: RED: RTL/unit tests for options, active state, disabled Phase 2 entries, success/error, unsupported locale and fallback display.
GREEN: build picker/action using existing next-intl and user profile primitives.
Run targeted i18n/component tests plus typecheck/lint for changed package.
risk_red_lines: Do not implement Phase 2 language content as part of this task.
Do not require full page reload for PL/EN switching.
Do not update organization locale when only user preference is changed.
Do not hard-code language state outside the app i18n primitives.
